addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

You are an agent working on the **bim-ai** repo (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).
This prompt is self-contained — start here.

---

## Repo orientation

```
packages/core/src/index.ts                          — Element union + ElemKind
packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx                — main plan interaction handler
packages/web/src/plan/gripProtocol.ts               — grip interaction protocol
packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx                 — grip rendering
packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts    — dispatches plan symbols per element
packages/web/src/plan/symbology.ts                  — plan symbol primitives
packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts              — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts               — per-tool grammar state machines
```

Tests: co-located `*.test.ts` — run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.

---

## What wave 1 already built — DO NOT rebuild these

- `interior_elevation_marker` element type in `@bim-ai/core` with fields:
  `positionMm`, `levelId`, `radiusMm`, `elevationViewIds` (N/S/E/W)
- `'interior-elevation'` tool (hotkey `IE`) in toolRegistry.ts, wired in PlanCanvas.tsx
- Plan symbol: 4-quadrant circle with inward arrows in `symbology.ts` (D2)
- A1–A12 grammar shells complete in `toolGrammar.ts`
- `permanent_dimension` element type in `core/index.ts` with `witnessPoints`, `valueMm`

---

## Tasks

### A — Interior elevation marker inspector + drag grip

**A1. Inspector panel**: When an `interior_elevation_marker` element is selected, the
Inspector panel should render:
- `radiusMm` — number input (mm), dispatches `{ type: 'updateElement', id, patch: { radiusMm } }`
- `levelId` — dropdown from project levels (study how other element inspectors do this)

Study how `text_note` or `spot_elevation` inspector panels are structured (search the
Inspector component for existing annotation inspectors added in wave 1).

**A2. Drag grip**: In `gripProtocol.ts` (or the grip registration pattern used by wave-1
A1/A2 grips), register a grip for `interior_elevation_marker` that:

### B — EQ condition on aligned dimensions (Ch. 4.2.2, P2)

**B1. Data model**: Add `eqEnabled?: boolean` to the `permanent_dimension` element type
in `core/index.ts`.

**B2. Plan rendering**: In the plan dimension renderer (find where `permanent_dimension`
elements are drawn — search `planElementMeshBuilders.ts` or `planProjection.ts`):
- When a selected `permanent_dimension` has ≥3 witness lines, render a small "EQ"
  button/label above the midpoint of the full dimension span
- When `eqEnabled: true`, display "EQ" instead of individual segment values, and
  distribute witness spacings equally (recompute positions for visual display only —
  do not move actual geometry)

**B3. Interaction**: Clicking the "EQ" label toggles `eqEnabled` via
`{ type: 'updateElement', id, patch: { eqEnabled: !current } }`.

- EQ label appears on a permanent_dimension with 3 segments, not 2
- When eqEnabled=true, rendered text shows "EQ" not individual values

Update tracker §4.2.2: "Partial — EQ toggle and visual display implemented"

1	/**
2	 * ANN-01/ANN-16 — grip providers for view-local annotation elements.
3	 *
4	 * text_note: single position grip to drag the whole label.
5	 * leader_text: anchor grip + text-block grip (move independently).
6	 */
7	import type { Element } from '@bim-ai/core';
8	
9	import type {
10	  ElementGripProvider,
11	  GripCommand,
12	  GripDescriptor,
13	  PlanContext,
14	} from '../gripProtocol';
15	
16	export type TextNote = Extract<Element, { kind: 'text_note' }>;
17	export type LeaderText = Extract<Element, { kind: 'leader_text' }>;
18	
19	export const textNoteGripProvider: ElementGripProvider<TextNote> = {
20	  grips(el: TextNote, _ctx: PlanContext): GripDescriptor[] {
21	    const positionGrip: GripDescriptor = {
22	      id: `${el.id}:position`,
23	      positionMm: el.positionMm,
24	      shape: 'square',
25	      axis: 'free',
26	      hint: 'Drag to move text',
27	      onDrag: () => ({ kind: 'unknown', id: el.id }),
28	      onCommit: (delta): GripCommand => ({
29	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
30	        elementId: el.id,
31	        key: 'positionMm',
32	        value: JSON.stringify({
33	          xMm: el.positionMm.xMm + delta.xMm,
34	          yMm: el.positionMm.yMm + delta.yMm,
35	        }),
36	      }),
37	      onNumericOverride: (absoluteMm): GripCommand => ({
38	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
39	        elementId: el.id,
40	        key: 'positionMm',
41	        value: JSON.stringify({ xMm: absoluteMm, yMm: el.positionMm.yMm }),
42	      }),
43	    };
44	    return [positionGrip];
45	  },
46	};
47	
48	export const leaderTextGripProvider: ElementGripProvider<LeaderText> = {
49	  grips(el: LeaderText, _ctx: PlanContext): GripDescriptor[] {
50	    const anchorGrip: GripDescriptor = {
51	      id: `${el.id}:anchor`,
52	      positionMm: el.anchorMm,
53	      shape: 'circle',
54	      axis: 'free',
55	      hint: 'Drag to move leader anchor',
56	      onDrag: () => ({ kind: 'unknown', id: el.id }),
57	      onCommit: (delta): GripCommand => ({
58	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
59	        elementId: el.id,
60	        key: 'anchorMm',
61	        value: JSON.stringify({
62	          xMm: el.anchorMm.xMm + delta.xMm,
63	          yMm: el.anchorMm.yMm + delta.yMm,
64	        }),
65	      }),
66	      onNumericOverride: (absoluteMm): GripCommand => ({
67	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
68	        elementId: el.id,
69	        key: 'anchorMm',
70	        value: JSON.stringify({ xMm: absoluteMm, yMm: el.anchorMm.yMm }),
71	      }),
72	    };
73	    const textGrip: GripDescriptor = {
74	      id: `${el.id}:text`,
75	      positionMm: el.textMm,
76	      shape: 'square',
77	      axis: 'free',
78	      hint: 'Drag to move text block',
79	      onDrag: () => ({ kind: 'unknown', id: el.id }),
80	      onCommit: (delta): GripCommand => ({
81	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
82	        elementId: el.id,
83	        key: 'textMm',
84	        value: JSON.stringify({
85	          xMm: el.textMm.xMm + delta.xMm,
86	          yMm: el.textMm.yMm + delta.yMm,
87	        }),
88	      }),
89	      onNumericOverride: (absoluteMm): GripCommand => ({
90	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
91	        elementId: el.id,
92	        key: 'textMm',
93	        value: JSON.stringify({ xMm: absoluteMm, yMm: el.textMm.yMm }),
94	      }),
95	    };
96	    return [anchorGrip, textGrip];
97	  },
98	};

You are implementing Wave 2 WP-A for the bim-ai repo at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai.
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).
Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write — no manual formatting needed.

## Context

You are in a git worktree. Before doing anything, run:
  git pull --rebase origin main

## What exists already

- `interior_elevation_marker` element type in `packages/core/src/index.ts` at line ~2938:
  fields: `id`, `positionMm: XY`, `levelId: string`, `radiusMm?: number`, `elevationViewIds: { north, south, east, west }`
- Grip provider pattern: see `packages/web/src/plan/grip-providers/annotationGripProvider.ts` — textNoteGripProvider uses `updateElementProperty` command with `key: 'positionMm'` and `value: JSON.stringify(...)`. This is the canonical pattern.
- Inspector pattern: see `packages/web/src/workspace/inspector/InspectorContent.tsx` — `case 'spot_elevation':` at line ~2146 and `case 'text_note':` at line ~1978 show the exact pattern with `onPropertyChange` callback and number inputs.
- Grip registry: `packages/web/src/plan/grip-providers/index.ts` — add the new provider to `gripsFor()` switch.
- Existing test for the marker: `packages/web/src/plan/interiorElevationMarker.test.ts`

## What does NOT exist (must be created)

- `permanent_dimension` element type — the wave-1 prompt claimed it was built but it isn't. You must add it.
- Inspector case for `interior_elevation_marker`
- Grip provider for `interior_elevation_marker`

---

## Task A — Interior elevation marker inspector + drag grip

### A1. Inspector panel

In `packages/web/src/workspace/inspector/InspectorContent.tsx`, add a case for `'interior_elevation_marker'`:
- Show `radiusMm` number input (mm) — on blur dispatch `updateElementProperty` with `key: 'radiusMm'`, `value: String(newNumber)`. Use the `onPropertyChange` callback (same as spot_elevation does).
- Show `levelId` as a read-only FieldRow (same as spot_elevation shows hostViewId). Study the `case 'spot_elevation':` block for the exact JSX structure.
- Use `data-testid="inspector-interior-elevation-radius"` on the input.

### A2. Drag grip

Create `packages/web/src/plan/grip-providers/interiorElevationMarkerGripProvider.ts`:
- Export `interiorElevationMarkerGripProvider: ElementGripProvider<InteriorElevationMarker>`
- Single grip at `el.positionMm`, shape `'circle'`, axis `'free'`, hint `'Drag to move elevation marker'`
- `onCommit(delta)` returns:
  ```ts
  {
    type: 'updateElementProperty',
    elementId: el.id,
    key: 'positionMm',
    value: JSON.stringify({ xMm: el.positionMm.xMm + delta.xMm, yMm: el.positionMm.yMm + delta.yMm }),
  }
  ```
- `onDrag()` returns `{ kind: 'unknown', id: el.id }` (same pattern as textNoteGripProvider)
- `onNumericOverride(absoluteMm)` returns the property update with `xMm: absoluteMm, yMm: el.positionMm.yMm`

Then in `packages/web/src/plan/grip-providers/index.ts`:
- Import `interiorElevationMarkerGripProvider`
- Add `case 'interior_elevation_marker': return interiorElevationMarkerGripProvider.grips(element, context);` to `gripsFor()`

### Tests for Task A

Create `packages/web/src/plan/grip-providers/interiorElevationMarkerGripProvider.test.ts`:
```ts
// Test 1: gripsFor returns one circle grip at positionMm
// Test 2: onCommit(delta) returns updateElementProperty with correct new positionMm
```

Create or append to `packages/web/src/workspace/inspector/InspectorContent.test.tsx` (look at existing tests there for the pattern):
- Test that rendering with an `interior_elevation_marker` element shows `data-testid="inspector-interior-elevation-radius"` with the current `radiusMm` value.

---

## Task B — EQ condition on aligned dimensions

### B1. Data model

In `packages/core/src/index.ts`, after the `interior_elevation_marker` element type (around line 2946), add a new element type to the `Element` union and to the `ElemKind` union:

```ts
| {
    /**
     * ANN-P2 — permanent aligned dimension chain with N witness points.
     * EQ mode drives all segments to equal spacing (visual only).
     */
    kind: 'permanent_dimension';
    id: string;
    levelId: string;
    /** Ordered witness points (plan mm). Must have ≥2 points. */
    witnessPointsMm: XY[];
    /** Offset of dimension line from the witness point chain, in mm. */
    offsetMm: XY;
    /** When true, display "EQ" instead of individual segment values. */
    eqEnabled?: boolean;
  }
```

Also add `'permanent_dimension'` to the `ElemKind` string union (search for `| 'interior_elevation_marker'` in the union at the top of the file — it's around line 372 — and add `| 'permanent_dimension'` right after it).

### B2. Plan rendering

Find the file that renders `'dimension'` elements in plan — search for `case 'dimension':` in `packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts` or `packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts`.

Add a `case 'permanent_dimension':` block that:
1. Draws witness lines between each adjacent pair of `witnessPointsMm`
2. When `el.eqEnabled` is false (or undefined): renders each segment's length as a text label at the segment midpoint
3. When `el.eqEnabled` is true: renders "EQ" as a single text label at the midpoint of the full span (first-to-last witness point)
4. When the element is selected AND has ≥3 witness points: renders a small "EQ" button/label above the midpoint of the full span that, when clicked, dispatches `{ type: 'updateElement', id: el.id, patch: { eqEnabled: !el.eqEnabled } }` — look at how interactive labels are done in the plan renderer

If the plan renderer doesn't support interactive labels easily, implement a simpler version: just change the displayed text to "EQ" when eqEnabled=true. The interactive EQ toggle can be a separate affordance in the inspector.

### B3. Inspector for permanent_dimension

In `InspectorContent.tsx`, add a `case 'permanent_dimension':` block:
- Show segment count: `FieldRow label="Segments" value={String(el.witnessPointsMm.length - 1)}`
- Show EQ toggle button: when `onPropertyChange` is available, render a button "EQ" that dispatches `onPropertyChange('eqEnabled', !el.eqEnabled)`. Use `data-testid="inspector-permanent-dimension-eq"`.

### Tests for Task B

Create `packages/web/src/plan/permanentDimension.test.ts`:
- Test that a `permanent_dimension` with 3 witness points and `eqEnabled: false` — rendered text is NOT "EQ"
- Test that a `permanent_dimension` with `eqEnabled: true` — rendered text is "EQ"
- Test that a `permanent_dimension` with 2 witness points (1 segment) does NOT show EQ affordance

---

Find `#### 6.1.5 Innenansichten` — change the last sentence from "Inspector panel and drag-grip for placed markers are Not Started." to "Inspector panel (radius, levelId) and drag-grip implemented in wave 2 WP-A." Change status line to: `**Status: Partial — D2 (inspector+grip done; full elevation-view rendering is separate)**`

2. Find `#### 4.2.2 EQ-Bedingung` — update status to: `**Status: Partial — P2 (EQ toggle and visual display implemented)**` and update the body to describe what was built.

---

## Commit + push

- Run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before committing
- Commit all changes with message: `feat(WP-A): interior elevation inspector+grip, EQ condition on aligned dimensions`
- Push to origin

## Rules

- Do NOT touch toolRegistry.ts, IFC/DXF export, curtain wall, group renderer
- Do NOT remove or change existing element types or their existing fields
- Keep tests co-located with the code they test
- All TypeScript must compile — check for type errors

     2	 * D2 — grip provider for interior_elevation_marker elements.
     3	 *
     4	 * Single circle grip at positionMm to drag the whole marker.

     6	import type { Element } from '@bim-ai/core';
     7	
     8	import type {
     9	  ElementGripProvider,
    10	  GripCommand,
    11	  GripDescriptor,
    12	  PlanContext,
    13	} from '../gripProtocol';
    14	
    15	export type InteriorElevationMarker = Extract<Element, { kind: 'interior_elevation_marker' }>;
    16	
    17	export const interiorElevationMarkerGripProvider: ElementGripProvider<InteriorElevationMarker> = {
    18	  grips(el: InteriorElevationMarker, _ctx: PlanContext): GripDescriptor[] {
    19	    const positionGrip: GripDescriptor = {
    20	      id: `${el.id}:position`,
    21	      positionMm: el.positionMm,
    22	      shape: 'circle',
    23	      axis: 'free',
    24	      hint: 'Drag to move elevation marker',
    25	      onDrag: () => ({ kind: 'unknown', id: el.id }),
    26	      onCommit: (delta): GripCommand => ({
    27	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
    28	        elementId: el.id,
    29	        key: 'positionMm',
    30	        value: JSON.stringify({
    31	          xMm: el.positionMm.xMm + delta.xMm,
    32	          yMm: el.positionMm.yMm + delta.yMm,
    33	        }),
    34	      }),
    35	      onNumericOverride: (absoluteMm): GripCommand => ({
    36	        type: 'updateElementProperty',
    37	        elementId: el.id,
    38	        key: 'positionMm',
    39	        value: JSON.stringify({ xMm: absoluteMm, yMm: el.positionMm.yMm }),
    40	      }),
    41	    };
    42	    return [positionGrip];
    43	  },
    44	};
